provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from (a) statutory PAYGO scorecards under section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010, (b) Senate PAYGO scorecards maintained for purposes of section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress), and (c) budget scorekeeping classifications under section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, allocations under section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, and paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
notes: Standard budget-neutrality exemption provision for Division B
Subsection (a) exempts from statutory PAYGO scorecards per the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Subsection (b) exempts from Senate PAYGO scorecards per H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)

Subsection (c) exempts from three additional scorekeeping mechanisms: (1) section 251 of Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, (2) section 302(a) allocations of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974, and (3) paragraph (4)(C) of section 3 of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Notwithstanding Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines from Conference Report 105-217 and section 250(c)(8) of BBEDCA
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 2401. 
Budgetary effects
(a) 
Statutory paygo scorecards
The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
provisions.title: IV

flagged_issues: SEC. 101 contains 10 CR substitutions reducing supplemental amounts from Divisions M and N of P.L. 117-328
SEC. 124 allows $621,270,000 for Columbia Class Submarine notwithstanding DoD new-start limitations
SEC. 126 increases OPM Salaries and Expenses from $190,784,000 to $219,076,000 above FY2023 baseline
SEC. 129 provides $16,000,000,000 emergency supplemental for FEMA Disaster Relief Fund
SEC. 133 contains an indefinite appropriation ('such sums as may be necessary') for Social Security Act activities — not included in total_budget_authority
SEC. 137 contains offsetting substitutions for FAA F&E: +$47M general fund / -$47M trust fund, net zero change
Section 521(d)(1) CR substitution in SEC. 101(2) lacks a named account — likely a CJS general provision (possibly Crime Victims Fund)
SEC. 2101(a)(2) contains an indefinite appropriation (pro rata of FY2023 amounts) — no specific dollar figure extractable.
SEC. 2101(b) extends federal programs/services with open-ended duration (until new agreement enters into force), unlike the rest of the CR which ends Nov 17, 2023.
All dollar amounts represent contract authority from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund (mandatory spending), not discretionary appropriations
All program extensions cover the period October 1 through December 31, 2023 (Q1 FY2024)
SEC. 2221 sets Counter-UAS authority to expire November 18, 2023, before the CR general expiration date

Title III as provided contains only Subtitle A (Animal Drug and Animal Generic Drug User Fee Amendments); Chapter 2 may be incomplete as it appears to be missing savings clause, effective date, and sunset date sections analogous to SEC. 2305-2307 in Chapter 1
Dollar amounts ($33,500,000 for ADUFA and $25,000,000 for AGDUFA) represent annual user fee revenue targets authorized to be collected from industry, not direct appropriations from the Treasury
AGDUFA fee revenue increased significantly from $18,336,340 to $25,000,000 per year
New generic investigational new animal drug file fee of $50,000 per file established in SEC. 2312(c)
SEC. 2342 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund from $7,000,000,000 to $6,357,117,810, a net reduction of $642,882,190 serving as a budget offset
SEC. 2351 contains an indefinite appropriation ('such sums as may be necessary') with no specific dollar amount
SEC. 2352 and SEC. 2353 provide pro-rata funding for Oct 1 – Nov 17, 2023 but do not specify explicit dollar amounts in the text
All extensions in this title run through November 17, 2023, matching the duration of the continuing resolution in Division A
